name: Pennsylvania German (Pennsylvania Dutch)
notes: Pennsylvania German / Pennsylvania Dutch (Pennsilfaanisch Deitsch), a Palatine (Rhine Franconian) West Germanic variety of North America. COMMITMENT: this map implements ONE of the two rival writing systems — the BUFFINGTON-BARBA (later Buffington-Barba-Beam, 'BBB') orthography, which spells by Standard German conventions. The competing English-based spelling (which writes the same words by American English conventions, with no difference in pronunciation) is OUT OF SCOPE and will mis-transcribe here. INPUT CONTRACT: lowercase BBB-spelled Pennsylvania German. BBB letter values that differ from Standard German: <v> = /f/, <w> = the unrounded labial approximant /ʋ/, <y> = /j/ initially but /ɪ/ medially, <o> short = /ɔ/, <aa> = /ɔː/, <ei> = /aɪ/ while <ai> is also /aɪ/, <g> = /ɡ/ initially, /k/ finally and a palatal glide [j] medially, <r> is vocalised to [ɐ] after <e>. The IPA values here render the BBB pronunciation key (which is stated in English/German keywords, not IPA) against the IPA vowel inventory documented for the variety. <tz> = /ts/ follows from BBB's Standard-German spelling conventions (as does <z>). Unstressed <e> may also be the reduced /ə/ documented for the variety. Graphemes for which no BBB row exists (e.g. <ä>, <oo>, <eu>, <tsch>) are deliberately OMITTED rather than guessed. Phonologically the variety continues Palatine German: SG /œ øː yː/ -> /ɛ eː iː/, intervocalic /b/ -> /v/, post-vocalic /ɡ/ -> /j/, and final /n/ deletion.
